Review: The location is great, the rooftop infinity pool faces the Kuala Lumpur Tower and provides a view of part of the Petronas Towers. It would look stunning on a clear day with blue skies and white clouds. The apartment has high ceilings and a spacious dining and living area, making it quite comfortable to stay in. However, the small bedroom lacks windows, which affects natural light. Additionally, the rooftop restaurant is nice with great views and delicious food. The only downside was that after I paid with Alipay, the transfer to the restaurant was very slow, taking over an hour and slightly impacting my subsequent plans.

Review: The view from the swimming pool partially obscures the Twin Towers at Platinum2, but the pool view is still top-notch. The accommodation was spacious and comfortable, but it seems to retain an old feeling. If you don't consider it a luxury hotel and instead think about the spacious room and excellent pool view, it's really satisfactory for the price. Unlike the hotel, a separate caretaker manages the check-in and check-out process, and there’s a 500 ringgit deposit. I paid in cash and received an immediate refund upon checkout. I think I waited about 20 minutes for the room check at check-out. They respond to requests through KakaoTalk as well. It was a satisfying experience.
Hotel_Address: THE PLATINUM SUITES ,1020 Jalan Sultan Ismail, 50250 Kuala Lumpur, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50250

Review: I booked a studio with a king size bed, but instead of checking in at building A, I checked in at building B, which is adjacent to the road, and I received a much better and larger apartment with two rooms and two beds. I had a spacious living room and an island-style kitchen, and there was a children's room along with a private bathroom and shower room. I couldn't have asked for a better stay for six days. However, the swimming pool was a bit short, and the Petronas Towers were somewhat blocked from view due to the nearby building, but the view of the KL Tower and Merdeka Tower was great, and it was suitable for cooling off from the heat during the day. It took about 20 minutes to walk in slippers from Suria Mall to the Petronas Towers in the late afternoon, which was comfortable enough for some exercise. I had sushi and rolls for dinner and breakfast, and I had a great time. If I go back, I would want to return to this place because it was the best value accommodation during my 15-night stay in Malaysia.
Hotel_Address: THE PLATINUM SUITES ,1020 Jalan Sultan Ismail, 50250 Kuala Lumpur, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50250

Review: Honestly, at this price, this size and quality of accommodation? It doesn't make sense. The room is very spacious and feels like it could be suitable for parties, but it seems everyone thinks the same, so parties are prohibited. It’s not a hotel but a luxury residence being used like a hotel. So, check-in is done by staff at the lobby sofa on the first floor with a list to check names (not done at the reception). If you are not someone who frequently calls the reception or requests housekeeping, there are no major inconveniences. The infinity pool is also very beautiful and nice (it’s quite long, which is good for swimming, and has a great view). The accommodation is extremely good and spacious for the price, so I definitely plan to revisit. However, it's a bit disappointing that a new building next door blocks the view of the famous tower, but it's still good value for money. The room has floor-to-ceiling windows and includes a shoe cabinet (very spacious), kitchen, living room (sofa + TV + table), bedroom, bathroom, and dressing room, providing ample living space. However, since there is no desk, if you need to work during your stay on a business trip, you'll have to consider that. The floor-to-ceiling windows offer a very nice view in the morning and at night. Highly recommended!
Hotel_Address: THE PLATINUM SUITES ,1020 Jalan Sultan Ismail, 50250 Kuala Lumpur, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50250

Review: 500 MYR safety deposit and too many rules. So I was stressed whether I am breaking any rules or not. Other than that tue room is awesome, great view. Has everything in the room. The swimming pool has great view but too much chlorine. Its a busy building but 24/7 staff at ground flood.

Review: Located in Platinum Suites just right beside The Face Suites (different building and reception—always ask). I was waiting for almost 30 mins to check in as it was peak holiday season but the receptionist was cooperative. In case you wonder, they will ask you deposit around RM500 in cash (returned when check out) or credit card (will be returned in weeks depending on issuer). I was assigned to 46th floor unit and surprised by the huge room with huge windows allow you to overview KL skyline. Location itself was quite strategic, walking distance to MRL Bukit Nanas just 2 stations away from Bukit Bintang and you can take 15 mins car ride to KL Sentral. What I appreciate the most is their service. I was troubled with the washing machine and told the staff to help with it. After 20 mins of waiting, they come with a new washing machine to replace the broken one and it works fine. All done with no charge. It also provides complete kitchen set, water heater, iron + board and hair dryer. I recommend you to try this property!
Hotel_Address: THE PLATINUM SUITES ,1020 Jalan Sultan Ismail, 50250 Kuala Lumpur, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50250

Review: Hotel is walking distance from Bukit Nanas Monorail and 10mins walking distance to cafes and other tourist spots like the Petronas Twin Tower. Room was super clean and spacious, and the bathtub was a surprise. Would definitely re-stay with them again.

Review: The location is convenient, and the unit has good potential; however, there are several areas that require improvement. The lift was extremely slow, which caused inconvenience. The water heater took more than 30 minutes to warm up, and the refrigerator was not cold. In addition, the surrounding area is very noisy due to frequent motorcycle traffic, and soundproof windows would greatly improve the overall comfort of the stay. Overall, maintenance and housekeeping need better attention to ensure a more comfortable stay.

Review: The booking process through Agoda was streamless and efficient. Quite hassle-free. The location was very nice. Right in the middle of the city and tourist spots. The unit was superb. It is very spacious and perfect for families. The cleanliness was commendable. It was fully furnished and comfortable. The only catch was the receptionist in charge of checking us in and checking us out. They did not have a real desk to entertain guests., They merely had a ledge and a sofa. Not very hospitable. They would benefit from a basic hospitality course. Speaks too fast and even got irked when I requested that my elevator key card be recalibrated since it wasn't functioning. Upon check out they said it would take 30 minutes to check my room. I was literally the only one who checked out 3 hrs before the standard time. Only when I said our van is already in the parking lot did they heed my request. After 5 minutes they said the checking was done and I was clear.30 minutes versus 5 minutes for two staff who were just sitting in the sofa, with no guests, busy with their cellphones. This was the only thing that needs improvement.
Hotel_Address: THE PLATINUM SUITES ,1020 Jalan Sultan Ismail, 50250 Kuala Lumpur, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50250

Review: For this hotel-apartment experience, there are some points which were lacking. Although for solo travelling, it is my diligence to always be well-prepared for such situations. Check-in process may be **tedious for those who are not mobile-savvy**. Upon entering the room, some amenities were missing (not refilled) and the room smelled very musty (room probably had been left vacant for awhile) as the room itself was so far inside the D zone of the building - where lifts at that area were inaccessible. Had to communicate w the Agent-Service team via WhatsApp to get the items that were lacking which took some time. I liked the spaciousness of the room. Bath water temp was not warm enough though. Agent-provider should have some Room Sanitizers/deodorants for better comfort of guests and better water-heating. Prime location located in the heart of the City. (Agoda - Yet to receive back my deposit of 500RM)

Review: The only thing that annoyed me for this hotel stay was the check in where unlike other hotels I used to stay with my partner I had to make sure the booking name was the same as the passport and my booking wasn't and there was so much delay having to call Agoda's customer service to change it after reaching late due to traffic and my baby was tired and my helper, me and my husband were all hungry and tired. Other than that all was quite good as the shelf was broken before we stayed and the customer service for the hotel was kind enough to fix it without charging us and housekeeping was just a text away. They also fixed the shower head without charging us as it was broken before we stayed also. They also helped us with the washing machine when my helper first used it and wasn't sure how it works.

Review: I'm not sure if all of these rooms are the same but mine and my friends rooms were more like an apartment and not a hotel room which is fine. They are very large. However, basic things like hand towels and hand soap do not appear to be standard here. This is clearly a much older building and you can tell by the wear and tear and cleanliness or lack thereof. They have a pool, however there does not seem to be any towels for the pool. And a very strange practice for me, which I have not experienced before and I have traveled the world is the necessity for your name and your Agoda booking to be exactly like that in your passport. Apparently for security's sake, which makes no sense given there's a photo of you and your first and last name.
Hotel_Address: THE PLATINUM SUITES ,1020 Jalan Sultan Ismail, 50250 Kuala Lumpur, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50250

Review: Absolutely not worth it. The hotel only gives free parking for ONE car, and the second car needs to pay RM15 for just 3 hours. What a joke. And the parking isn’t even at the same block as the room, so imagine dragging bags and walking far while bringing an elderly person. great “hospitality”, right? For RM300 a night, room just ok. Nothing special, nothing memorable. except the disappointment. The whole place feels more like an overcrowded foreign hostel. pool full of foreigners, lifts full, lobby full. If you’re Malaysian, be prepared to feel like a tourist in your own country, except worse - because you’re treated like one. The highlight of the nonsense? I asked the receptionist a simple question in Malay about where the lift was. She replied, “English please.” Wow. In MALAYSIA. A Malaysian guest speaking the national language… and still gets told to speak English. If that’s the standard here, maybe they should just change the flag at the entrance too. Overpriced, overrated, and over-attitude. Malaysians, you deserve better. Skip this place unless you enjoy paying premium prices to be disrespected in your own country.
Hotel_Address: THE PLATINUM SUITES ,1020 Jalan Sultan Ismail, 50250 Kuala Lumpur, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50250

Review: The room is very large, with good sound insulation, and there are two bathrooms, making it very convenient for our family. I really like the kitchen, which provides good utensils, but there are no chopsticks, making it a bit inconvenient to cook instant noodles. There is also a more serious issue; the non-stick pan is too old, with many scratches, making it unsafe to use, so I strongly recommend replacing it. I also hope that future guests will take care of the cookware. The rooftop pool and gym make the vacation experience more enjoyable, but the elevator lacks ventilation, and many guests crowd together, making it stuffy. Lastly, I would like to thank the beautiful lady at the front desk and the staff who cleaned our room, for ensuring we had a smooth and comfortable vacation experience.
Hotel_Address: THE PLATINUM SUITES ,1020 Jalan Sultan Ismail, 50250 Kuala Lumpur, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50250

Review: I chose this hotel based on its 8.3 “Excellent” rating, but unfortunately, my experience didn’t live up to the expectations. Parking was a nightmare – Traffic and parking situation is very poorly managed. The area around the hotel is extremely congested, with cars stopping and waiting everywhere, making it very difficult to get through. Do note that you will need to check in first and get the access card for you to get free parking. Cleanliness needs improvement – the room wasn’t properly cleaned, with visible stains on the sofa and smelly towels provided. This left a bad first impression. Access issues – the hotel access card was faulty, and I had to rely on a physical key to enter the room. This was inconvenient and unacceptable for a hotel in this price range. The lift situation was frustrating – extremely long wait times to go up or down, especially during peak hours. This really tested my patience. Pool experience was unpleasant – the security staff at the pool were unnecessarily rude. You’re also required to bring your own towel if you want to access the pool area for a swim, which wasn’t made clear beforehand. If not, they won’t allow you to enter the pool area.. Overall, it was not a pleasant stay. There are serious operational and maintenance issues that need urgent attention. I hope management takes this feedback seriously.
Hotel_Address: THE PLATINUM SUITES ,1020 Jalan Sultan Ismail, 50250 Kuala Lumpur, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50250

Review: The room rates are among the most affordable in Kuala Lumpur city centre. Considering the price, it offers good value, especially if you are looking for a spacious room. However, the overall experience did not meet my expectations compared to what was shown in the photos. The room feels quite old, and the cleanliness needs improvement. One of the towels had what appeared to be blood stains (although it could have been red ink), which was quite unpleasant. In addition, the light above the room entrance kept blinking, making the room feel poorly maintained. The worst part of our stay was when the lift got stuck on the 48th floor for more than 20 minutes. I was carrying my baby at the time, which made the situation very stressful and frightening. While I understand that technical issues can happen, I hope the hotel takes lift maintenance more seriously to ensure the safety of its guests.

Review: Awesome place. Very spacious and big. It is a Service Apartment conjoined together with hotel. The lifts can be a big issue. Food and transport can very easily find you. Though because the face platinum suits 1 and 2 are nearby the time it takes for the grab to leave the place can take up some time but fret not there are security guards in place to drive the traffic flow and expedite it. Most importantly, if you want an unobstructed view of the twin towers got to the face platinum suites 2 not 1 because the 2 is the newer one. 1 is much older. Review: From check-in to check-out, the experience was exceptional. The room was beautifully appointed, the breakfast spread was extensive and delicious, and the swimming pool was excellent. Review: I was worried because there weren't many Korean reviews, but it was unnecessary. The hotel was very nice, and I was satisfied with the friendly and excellent service from the staff. The room and bathroom were spacious and well-maintained, and the automatic blinds provided a modern feel. The minibar in the room was fully complimentary, and I used the laundry service, which responded quickly. Most guests were from Europe or China, and there were only a few Koreans, but communication in English was smooth, so there were no issues. If I had to mention one downside, it would be that the area for stopping vehicles at the main entrance of the hotel is a bit narrow. Other than that, I was very satisfied overall and would love to visit again.

Review: Convenient to walk to Pavilion and KLCC. Nice ambience and excellent toilet set-up with double sinks, bathtub and separate wet & dry areas. Spacious room and nice swimming pool area too. Decent buffet breakfast. Staff all polite and friendly.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Book this place for my husband’s birthday staycation and i have no regrets. Room was spacious, calm and I requested for birthday decorations and they nailed it. Breakfast spread was superb too!
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Had a fantastic stay at The Ruma Hotel and Residence. The room was clean, cozy, and well-equipped with blackout curtains for a great night's sleep. The bathtub was spacious and perfect for relaxing. Free parking was a nice bonus, and the staff were friendly, helpful, and attentive throughout my stay. Would definitely come back again!
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Everything was perfect, from the services, facilities, the staff and all. The hotel even provided us complementary cake and decoration for our anniversary celebration. The chef also provided us complementary food during breakfast because he knew we were celebrating our anniversary. Thank you The Ruma!
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Literally the best hotel I’ve ever stayed at! The attention to detail is amazing! The decor, the service, the food, the staff…everything was excellent. It made us feel at home.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Had a truly wonderful stay at The RuMa Hotel and Residences. The service was warm, attentive and genuinely thoughtful from check-in to check-out. The rooms were beautifully designed, modern yet cosy, with excellent attention to detail and comfort. The location is perfect, especially if you want to be within walking distance of KLCC while still enjoying a peaceful, refined atmosphere. Facilities were top-notch and everything felt well maintained and premium. Highly recommended for both business and leisure stays. Would definitely return.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: The very best place to stay..we stay for our anniversary and request them to do some decorations..they come out with the deco and also the cake for us..really recommended place to stay
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Rooms were in good condition. Service was brilliant. Location is just couple of mins walk away from KLCC.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: We booked just for one night, but we felt so satisfied staying there. The moment we walked into the hotel, we were warmly greeted by the staff during check-in. At Level 6, we had such a beautiful night by the swimming pool while listening to the music from an event featuring Borneo-vibe songs. The unlimited drinks for just rm200 really makes my friend so happy. The room was very clean, and we absolutely loved the theme, decoration, and overall ambience. Again, a big shoutout to all the staff who were always smiling and asking if there was anything else they could help us with. It really made us feel so welcomed. We will definitely come back again.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Love the service from all staff - check in till check out. Breakfast was a good spread! Thank you for the hospitality throughout our stay. Will be back!
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: From the entrance, I am welcomed by the doormen, followed by a friendly check-in. The oriental decor scattered throughout the room makes me feel like I'm staying in a museum rather than a hotel. The pool and facilities match the reputation of a five-star establishment. One downside is that the quality of the breakfast is only at a four-star level. I would like to give this hotel a total score of 90 points.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: I had a splendid experience and stay in this hotel. From check-in to check-out, the staff were very helpful and friendly. They offered help before I even opened my mouth. The rooms are spacious and clean. The hotel itself offers privacy and a quiet stay. It has its own calm and charm. I will definitely come back.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Have an awesome weekend at The Ruma Hotel. Start from check in after cheek out everything was perfect. The room so wonderful and breakfast buffet yummy.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Although we only stayed for one night, the experience exceeded our expectations. Was celebrating my partner’s birthday, and the hotel team went above and beyond by decorating the room with balloons, preparing a complimentary birthday cake, and leaving a thoughtful handwritten card. These little touches made our stay feel memorable. The hotel is also within walking distance of KLCC, making it an excellent choice for anyone looking to shop or explore the city. A special thank you to Aleyssa for coordinating everything so seamlessly and making our celebration so memorable. Would definitely stay here again the next time we’re in KL and highly recommend this hotel for special occasions or even a relaxing city getaway.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Easy walk to Pavilion and KLCC. Room is large and toilet feels grand with bathtub. Moderate amount of channel but Sports premier league channel is there. Didn't try the breakfast buffet as it was quite expensive to purchase at the hotel itself. Minibar drinks is wonderful and complimentary. The staff is very helpful as well.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: This is one of the best hotels near to klcc and pavilion. Very aesthetically pleasing and calming. Staff are very helpful.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: The location is great, right in the city center, just a few hundred meters from the subway station, Pavilion, and the Petronas Twin Towers, all within walking distance. The hotel's decor and layout are very tasteful, the service is also excellent, and the rooms are quite spacious. I brought my parents here, and they both thought it was pretty good. Next time we come to Malaysia, this will definitely be our first choice.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: This design hotel is very beautiful. Everything is very well done staff is very helpful. Food is very good. Location is excellent within walking distance to downtown area with all shoppings and amenities.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Excellent customer service. Check in was a breeze as they approached you and collected details and even offer some welcome drinks. Room is fantastic and feels homely. Hotel ambience feels very exclusive. Overall, fantastic stay!
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Stayed for 1 night in a corner room with view of the towers. Staff were really friendly, room was great, service great and lovely breakfast
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Immaculate cleanliness for room. Five star service from the moment i entered lobby till dining in the ATAS cafe. Friendly staff and amazing hospitality. Not to mention walking distance to both KLCC and pavillion. Highly recommended.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Worth what i am paying for!

Review: The hotel is well designed. The room is comfortable and clean. The location is near and walkable to Pavilion and KLCC. The breakfast is variety and nice. I would consider stay here again when I visit KL.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Complimentary parking, staff was courteous and accommodating to guest needs. Check-in was smooth using the pre-arrival checking in link. Entrance and lobby is nice and welcoming. Room is exceptional which my wife totally felt in a luxury home. Breakfast was delicious. Thank you for the hospitality and definitely i will return.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Had a fantastic 4-night stay at The RuMa Hotel! 😍 The staff were incredibly warm, friendly, and professional - made checking in and out a breeze. Location is unbeatable - major malls like Pavilion KL and Petronas Twin Towers are a short walk away, and Grab's always a tap away. Value-wise, the luxury here is a steal! Spacious rooms, elegant decor, and top-notch amenities like the outdoor pool, gym, spa, and multiple dining options - all at an affordable price. Whether you're here for business or leisure, The RuMa's got you covered. Highly recommend! 👍.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Everything was amazing! We had such a wonderful stay. Staff were really friendly, hospitable and gave really great service. We felt very comfortable there. As for facilities, we only managed to go for the morning breakfast and the buffet spread was great and really yummy. Honestly my fav part of the room is the bathroom. It was really clean and decorated beautifully. I love how clean the bathtub is!! Location was great as well! We drove there and we managed to avoid the pavilion jam. Ample parking and easy validation of free parking. Really loved our stay and will def pick RuMa again! Thank you all for your hardwork and passion!
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: We recently enjoyed a delightful stay at Hotel RuMa KL. The staff were exceptionally helpful and provided seamless service—from an efficient email check-in to a thoughtful room upgrade that conveniently allowed for early arrival. The hotel was spotless and well-organized, and the delicious breakfast featured clearly labeled vegan options. It’s an excellent choice for families—offering spacious, welcoming surroundings, free parking, and a strong sense of customer care that honors your time. Perfect location in KL. Highly recommended
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: I had 3 night stay alone in this place. Location is good as you can just walk to Pavilion shopping mall and grab taxis are so easy to take. Breakfast selection is good but coffee quality could be improved. Service was fast and efficient . Pool bar view at night is magnificent and remind me Sevva old bar from Hong Kong Mandarin Hotel. Room size was huge and everything was great and also pillow didn’t break my neck which was plus. Found some hair in the bathroom but not a big deal . Room number 1125 had issue with drainage and engineer came fo fix immediately but still water stays and couldn’t fix 100% percent. Overall my stay was good and door men were always smiling and canvassing with customers in great manner. I will re stay in if I visit KL in the future. Thank you.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: This is a gem of a hotel. It’s situated a stones throw away from the KLCC and Pavillion Mall. Tastefully decorated, very clean and comfortable. Great swimming pool and friendly staff. Thanks for Rapu at breakfast for being so helpful and attentive. Highly recommended!
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: This is my first time here and we cannot wait to stay here again in a few months time. The room was spacious and luxurious. We had our towels changed every day and every request to the concierge was executed perfectly. But most importantly, we never had hotel staffs so friendly and welcoming. Traffic right outside the hotel is not so congested unlike other hotels. Although on the weekends it might get loud as thought there might be a club somewhere. So expect some noises around 2-3am. The break first was not bad too, sometimes lacking the finesse but the mood will set you well. Lastly, most if not all amenities are child friendly. If you drive, parking is free at all times.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Finally got to visit The RuMa Hotel to celebrate a special occasion with my partner. Check in and check out were swift and all the staffs are so accommodating, welcoming and friendly. We got upgraded to the grand king room from deluxe king and was presented a complimentary cake for our celebration. We also added on the In House Privilege program which was really top notch! Food and drinks were great and free flow except alcoholic beverages. The tacos and sliders were our personal favourite. We spent quite some time at the gym too which was not very big but sufficient to do all my workouts comfortably. Staffs were all so attentive, they will be on standby to provide any assistance needed and giving out towels and water to all the guest on floor. Would definitely come back again. Shout out to the manager at Seven Lobby Bar, my partner and I felt so at ease and had great conversations that was a highlight of our evening.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Great location. Some views of the Petronas Towers from the pool but not brilliant. Easy to walk to them from the hotel though. Decor was amazing. Everything was presented beautifully, almost like an art gallery. The room was spacious and clean and bed was super comfortable. Facilities were good. Used the gym and pool and both did the job. The restaurant serves tasty food and bar has good selection of drinks. Staff all very pleasant if not a little too professional sometimes. The AC was pretty cold in the evening leading us to a quick retreat to bed. Would definitely stay here again though.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: My stay at The Ruma Hotel was truly exceptional from start to finish. Upon arrival, I was pleasantly surprised with a room upgrade, which set the tone for a wonderful experience. The staff were a highlight ,warm, professional, and genuinely helpful. Their attentiveness made me feel welcomed and cared for throughout my stay. Every interaction was kind and thoughtful, and their service felt both sincere and seamless. The hotel itself is beautifully designed, elegant and calming, offering a luxurious yet comfortable atmosphere. My upgraded room was spacious, spotless, and thoughtfully equipped. Breakfast was another standout ,delicious, fresh, and diverse, with a mix of local and international selections. It was the perfect way to start each day. The Ruma isn’t just a hotel, it’s a sanctuary. I would absolutely return and highly recommend it to anyone visiting KL.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: I had a wonderful stay. The location was perfect for exploring, the hotel was very clean, and the staff were extremely friendly and helpful. The bathroom was well maintained with plenty of hot water, which made it very comfortable. Overall it was excellent value for money and I would definitely recommend it.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: I enjoyed my stay in this hotel because of its great location and cleanliness. I could easily walk to KLCC and Pavilion from the hotel. The hotel staff vacuumed and mopped my room daily. It was great with air purifier to keep the air clean. The room was spacious. The drinks in the fridge were complimentary. They replenished them daily. The price paid might be high but it was considered value for money looking at the walking distance to KLCC and Pavilion, high level of cleanliness and complimentary drinks in the fridge daily. It was a hassles free stay for me.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Love everything about the RuMa for a romantic staycation with your partner! Room-big, beautiful, romantic vibe complete with he and she sinks and bathtub. Staffs-super friendly and welcoming from the moment you entering the premise, check in with the welcome drinks, during breakfast and at the pool. Received a complimentary cake for my honeymoon 🥺 Thanks guys! Location-in the heart of kl, walkable to anywhere-klcc, pavilion. 7/11 is 4mins walking towards kl convention centre. Parking-complimentary, within premise, just validate every time you want to go out at concierge. Overall, romantic , modern vibe with local Malaysian style (nasi lemak sambal sotong at breakfast is delicious)!! Will come back with my partner!!
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Our stay was truly exceptional! From the warm welcome at check-in to the attentive service throughout, every detail was thoughtfully taken care of. The room was spotless, spacious, and well-appointed, with all the comforts we needed. The atmosphere of the hotel was both elegant and relaxing, making it the perfect getaway. Special thanks to the staff for their professionalism and genuine hospitality — they really made us feel at home. Highly recommended for anyone seeking a memorable and outstanding stay
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Cleanliness is good, bathroom, bathtub water pressure was all great. Had a restful night. Their ATAS restaurant, however, is not up to expectation. Won't recommend. Otherwise, all is good.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: I cannot find a fault with this hotel. Excellent value for money and room was very big and comfortable. Had every room amenity needed for a comfortable stay with complimentary soft drinks and water topped up daily. The hotel staff were amazingly helpful and friendly in every part of the hotel. The pool was lovely to cool off whenever you were too hot. The location is perfect, central to everything you need to see in KL and use the Grab app (equivalent of Uber) to get around to sites a little further away for very cheap so you don't have to walk in the heat. I would definitely stay again when in KL!
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: I have a strong appreciation for art, and as a Malaysian, I am genuinely proud to introduce this hotel to tourists visiting Kuala Lumpur. The room is thoughtfully designed, with many lovely details that make the stay feel special—though the in-room snackbar could be more refined. The staff are polite and attentive, but the real highlight for me was the breakfast spread. It was exceptional. From the teh tarik corner to the nasi lemak, kuih-muih etc. it truly showcased our local culture and made me feel incredibly proud to be Malaysian. The pool and gym were equally impressive. I worked out during my stay and found the gym fully equipped, with thoughtful touches such as apples, chilled boxed water, and towels readily available. An amazing experience overall—amazing, amazing, amazing. I will definitely be back!
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: The Ruma Hotel was the absolute highlight of our trip to KL. The staff was friendly and helpful, the room was clean and spacious. The breakfast spread was amazing, and the quality was superb. Definitely recommend this hotel. The spa was great as well. While the location is really convenient, walking distance to KLCC and pavilion, the traffic can be really bad, so we suggest walking if possible
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: The room was clean, well kept, and very comfortable. Also very spacious for a single woman! The breakfast was buffet style and I really enjoyed the selections. A notable mention is that the staff were super friendly and accommodating; I asked for a vase to be sent to my room and they really did! Overall, the attention to detail and comfort of the property made this stay very memorable. Will return soon!
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: I have an overall amazing experience at The RuMa. Good customer service from the front desk to housekeeping. Breakfast buffet spread is not as extensive as other hotel, but everything is delicious and enjoyable. Very near to convention center (just across the road through sapura tower) and one of the good hotel option to stay if there are events near the convention. Room is very clean and spacious and overall just an enjoyable experience here!
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: We were greeted by Ming. Very polite, good customer service and made us feel welcome. I havent been to KL since covid. Ming definitely made us feel super welcome. Their service from my day 1 till my check out is top notch. Room is clean, cosy and has a very nice smell. There is only 1 issue for me. The bed is too soft. But thats an individual opinion. Always looking forward to be back to the room after a long day out. Really appreciate the small gesture where staff did the folded sheets at the corner with glass & plain water at the side table and blinds all down by the time we came back to the room in the evening. Preparing guest to have a “downtime”. Love love the ambiance of the whole concept of the hotel. Definitely coming back here if i were to visit KL again! Location is great! Walking distance to KLCC and pavi. Good job on your services Ruma! I really applaud u guys for doing a great service!
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Overall very lovely stay, wished we were there longer! Room was great, bathroom was even better. Service was tip top and breakfast selection was awesome. Location also very ideal as it's about a 5 minutes walk to Pavillion/ Bukit Bintang on one side and then to Suria KLCC on the other. Pool was nice but small, lounging area nice and cozy with good amount of seating. Wished the pool opened to at least 10pm instead of 9pm though. Family and friends have stayed before so definitely recommend it to anyone! A top choice for a very worthy rate.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: We had an exceptional stay at The RuMa Hotel and Residences, Kuala Lumpur, and it truly exceeded all expectations. From the moment we arrived, the service was flawless, warm, attentive, and genuinely thoughtful at every touchpoint. The staff were outstanding: professional, friendly, and always eager to help, making us feel completely at home. The food was excellent, beautifully presented, and consistently high quality. The facilities and amenities were equally impressive, luxurious, well-maintained, and clearly designed with guest comfort in mind. Every detail, from the rooms to the shared spaces, reflected elegance and care. Quite simply, we couldn’t fault a single thing about our stay. The RuMa sets a very high standard for hospitality, and we would not hesitate to return or recommend it to anyone visiting Kuala Lumpur.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: We had a really lovely time at Ruma Hotel. The staff were amazing, friendly and clearly well-trained. They really put a lot of thought in guests experience. Huge pluses were the early check-in, ( super grateful) the beautiful room with an incredible view, and the awesome breakfast spread. A few heads-up, especially for families: the walls and doors are a bit thin, so you can hear hallway noise and doors closing. The room wasn't fully soundproof, so we could hear activity from the hallway and neighboring doors. The main door lacks a soft-close mechanism, which resulted in some loud bangs. Also, the street noise at night with loud cars might wake up light sleepers (it did our baby!). The only real hiccup was our luggage taking a while because we didn't get luggage tags at check-in. ( which we weren’t informed/made aware) All in all, a fantastic hotel with superb service. Thank you for the memorable stay!
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Stay at RUMA
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Nice, clean hotel with friendly staff
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Fantastic
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: we are first time parents bringing our 5 month old for birthday staycation at the ruma. we’ve been to many hotels around kl before this, but let me tell u, the ruma is definitely the best so far. they kindly gave us a crib, baby bathtub n toilet seat, toiletries for baby evrything superb and complete. the staff were so friendly and warm, felt so confortable staying here. the housekeeping was another highlight, they didnt just clean the room, they folded the clothes and even arranged my washed breat pumps and baby bottles. my husband n i were surprised by their effort. it was so amazing. i can go on and on about how we enjoyed my stay. we would definitely be coming again. thank you so much to everyone at the ruma for making us feel so welcome. Well done and keep up the good work! no complaints at all everything perfect!!!👏🏻👏🏻👏🏻
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Our stay was exceptional. Staff was attentive. Breakfast buffet although not as big as other hotels, had many varieties. They had the turndown service to prepare for the night which was a pleasant surprise after a long day. Convenient location. Walking distance to Bukit Bintang, Pavillion, Suria KLCC via the bridge near the Convention Centre. Hotel was calm and very zen. Comfy bed and pillows. The bathtub was love. Will definitely be back! I had to deduct one star from the staff component. When we arrived, the hotel had a really nice smell. We wanted to know the type of fragrance used and asked the staff who was checking us in. He was surprised there was a smell and said it might be food smell. It was definitely not food.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Perfect for families
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: My stay at The RuMa Hotel was fantastic! The rooms were exceptionally clean and comfortable, with modern amenities and a great view of the city. The staff were incredibly friendly and accommodating, going above and beyond to ensure a pleasant experience. The location was perfect, within walking distance of major attractions and public transportation. The breakfast buffet had a wide variety of delicious options. Overall, a highly recommended stay for anyone visiting Kuala Lumpur.
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Honeymoon
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Newly decorated hotel
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Overall good but got room to improve
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Comfortable place, near Bukit Bintang, great friendly staff
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: Well located, discrete and clean. The room was well appointed and immaculate. We enjoyed the Library bar and extensive gin selection, as well as the pool bar with friends. Despite the diminutive exterior the hotel is surprisingly luxurious internally. The breakfast is a solid offering, and all staff are courteous and helpful. Very close to the Fahrenheit mall and 10 minutes on foot from a metro. Unfortunately did not have time to avail ourselves of the spa or Truefitt and Hill this time, will be back!
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: It was my 1st time at The Ruma Hotel. I choose Ruma based on reviews from Threads. I have zero expectation & wow! I was mind-blown by The Ruma. What makes my heart goes pitter-patter was the team & staffs of Ruma. My requests were fulfilled tremendously awesome by the team. All of them from the welcoming staff, receptionist, dining staff, room service staff and all of them. Thank you for making my mom & me happy. May all of you will be repay with prosperity & kindness too! #IWillBeBack please maintain this kind of service & cleanliness. Those will make us comeback 🫱🏻‍🫲🏼
Hotel_Address: 7 Jalan Kia Peng,, Kuala Lumpur City Centre, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 50450

Review: I recently stayed at this hotel, and it exceeded my expectations. From the moment I walked in, I was struck by the stunning interior design — elegant yet welcoming. Love the calm and inviting atmosphere. The service was exceptional. The staff went above and beyond to ensure my stay was comfortable and seamless. The room was absolutely beautiful — spacious and equipped with high-end amenities. I truly felt at home here and can't wait for my next visit. This hotel is a perfect blend of style, comfort and excellent service. Highly recommended!

Review: Really love the Ruma from the greetings to the facilities and cleanliness ! As for the downsides: although the room is beautiful, the items provided in the room reduce its overall value. For example, the kettle, cups, iron, etc., are of very poor quality and practically unusable. I find this quite disappointing. In addition, the housekeeping was not thorough, which made the room dusty in several areas, such as the headboard, inside the drawers, and low areas like the space for indoor slippers.

Review: I am very disappointed with my recent stay at The RuMa Hotel and Residences. Firstly, the noise level was unacceptable for a hotel of this standard. At around 10am, there were loud knocking sounds coming from the level above, which was very disruptive. More concerning, at approximately 2am, I could clearly hear doors being slammed repeatedly along the corridor, making it difficult to rest. My wife and I are not light sleepers, so you can imagine how loud and disturbing the noise must have been. Secondly, I attempted to lodge a complaint through the hotel’s WhatsApp channel during my stay, but unfortunately received no response at all. This lack of communication and support was extremely frustrating. Overall, the experience did not meet expectations in terms of comfort and service. I hope the management takes these issues seriously and improves both noise control and guest communication

I made an enquiry to see if a special rate could be extended for a future stay, considering the situation. The response mentioned that management had approved offering the iPrefer rate to me. However, based on that reply, I would have appreciated a more straightforward response, there’s no need to involve management approval for a standard rate like iPrefer. Review: My first time here at Sunway Pyramid Hotel and it has impressed me so much! The standards are impeccable indeed; very clean rooms and bathrooms, very safe and secure, and the best part is the fast accessibility to the mall! Makes this trip so worth it! Despite a minor hiccup with booking details between Agoda and the Hotel's records, the hotel resolved it quickly and managed to cater to our specific requests. Breakfast was delightful and offered a huge spread of local and international foods that satisfies any palate, with vegan and vegetarian options available. The Hotel sits right next to the themepark and it is quite an interesting place to spend time in with family, young and old. It even offers an actual beach in the middle of it, which has simulated waves for surfing! Amazing really. There is a wildlife zoo that features lions and tigers even, as well as a multitude of fun rides, including go-karting! A definite recommendation for any traveller coming to Subang Jaya for the first time.
Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500

Review: Was checking into the Sunway Pyramid Hotel and was very pleasantly surprised to find out that we were given an upgrade to the Sunway Resort! The staff were very helpful in bringing our luggage from one hotel to the other, check-in was smooth, and they offered complimentary cookies to our daughter. Our room was originally a deluxe room with a park view, so imagine our surprise that we still had the park view we wanted. The room was spacious and comfortable. The hotel was also connected to the mall so very convenient too. The breakfast was impressive and held a wide selection of cuisine, from local delights to Arabic food. The live cook station staff were also very friendly and overall food was delicious. The kids buffet section was a big hit, and very thoughtful of the hotel to have toddler sized tables and chairs. The hotel pool was spacious and also had a view of the Sunway Lagoon park. My daughter enjoyed the Waterventure pool on the 5th floor as well as the play area there. Overall, would definitely recommend staying there!

Review: A comfortable and clean hotel. The room is quite spacious, and the lobby is directly connected to Sunway Pyramid Mall. The location is very strategic and the service at the counter is very friendly. They are always attentive to the needs of customers and ready to provide service on time. I can see a beautiful view outside the window of the room. The colorful lights along the road are attractive. The only downside is that the location for the breakfast buffet requires a walk to the next building, Sunway Resort, to check in there. Luckily, there were staff who took the sign at the Sunway Resort lobby in the early morning to prevent getting lost or taking the wrong lift. This deserves praise. If you want to stay here, there's really no need to bring a car; it's easy to find food walking around. The cleanliness here is definitely top-notch, with staff always on duty anywhere.
Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500

Review: Since I have a annual pass for Sunway Lagoon, I prefer to stay at the hotel here instead of just making a day trip when I want to relax. The buffet also has a special area for children, with everything sized for them, which is adorable. For dinner, I either enjoy a meal at Pyramid Mall or buy a bento at Don Quijote to eat in my room. I hope to visit here often in the future.

It honestly feels like a mini theme park city. There’s a huge mall, water park, ice skating and so many food options, you’ll never feel bored or alone. I was sad that I only stayed for 2 nights because I didn’t realize how great the area was until I got there. Yes, the hotel is a bit old m, but I can’t complain at all. The room was spacious, very clean and had a desk which was perfect for using my laptop and getting some work done. Most of all, I felt safe and welcomed. The staff are friendly and kind. The hotel is literally a stone’s throw away from Sunway Lagoon, it’s also directly connected to Sunway Pyramid, so food & shopping are all just a short walk away — super convenient. The staff were very friendly and professional. Check-in was smooth and impressively fast, which is always a great start to a stay. I also managed to secure a late checkout. Toilet is very small. Breakfast cafe is so far away. Nothing much playing on TV. Staff members are nice and helpful. Room is big. Bed is huge and comfy. Location is great; wakking distance to everything. Maybe check out Sunway Resort instead.

Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500

The best part of the hotel was that its connected to the mall making it super convenient for us to shop and dine. The staff offered me a baby crib seeing me with my 1 yo baby. And, the breakfast spread was great. Will definitely coming back again!
Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500

Thank you for a memorable vacation for our family.

I wouldn’t hesitate to stay here again and would highly recommend it to anyone visiting the area.
Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500

The biggest advantage was its unbeatable location—just steps from Sunway Pyramid Mall, which made shopping, dining, and entertainment incredibly convenient, especially with kids in tow. The breakfast buffet was a highlight, offering a wide variety of tasty options that kept everyone happy each morning. The buffet breakfast spread was awesome! The food was delicious. I personally enjoyed their omelette. It would be much appreciated if I was told where the location of the hotel buffet was during check-in. Would be a nice proactive step for a welcoming stay. Despite that, I enjoyed the stay. It is highly recommended.
Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500

Review: It’s linked to the giant Sunway mall that has a supermarket, indoor playground, ice skating rink, arcade, and countless top restaurants, as well as Sunway lagoon theme park. Although Sunway pyramid hotel doesn’t have its own swimming pool and gym, it’s just a 5min walk through a shopping mall to Sunway Resort hotel’s which you can now use for free if you are a GHA member (free sign up as of writing). As a member you can also use Sunway Resort’s water playground for a nominal fee per child (as of writing). Having arrived late with young children we were very grateful for ***’s promptness and kindness at the check in counter as well as all the others at the concierge. The room we got was spacious, clean, and well maintained. Only one little snag was that it took about an hour for us to get a baby cot after we had checked in, but for the next two nights the room service staff attended to our needs within 5-10 minutes. Overall it was a super stay and we hope it’s price doesn’t go up from more people finding out about this gem
Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500

Review: I really enjoyed my stay at Sunway Pyramid Hotel. The best part is its unbeatable location — directly connected to the mall, making it so convenient to shop, dine, or just stroll around. There’s a wide variety of food choices, so you’ll never run out of options. The hotel itself is comfortable, clean, and well-managed, making the whole experience pleasant and hassle-free. A great choice for anyone who loves convenience and easy access to everything in one place!
Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500

Review: I enjoyed staying here, there are many facilities and the staff are friendly and kind. I booked 2 rooms with my family and requested a room with a connecting door, which was provided without any additional charge. Breakfast was indeed moved to Sunway Resort, but the location is not far as it was directed by the staff, and we just needed to go down the escalator, so it was not tiring. There was an abundance of food for breakfast to the point that we were confused about what to eat 😃. At checkout, we left our luggage because we wanted to go to Sunway Lagoon until the afternoon, and the staff helped us find transportation. Overall, all the staff were very helpful. The hotel is close to the mall, no need to go outside, in front of the hotel there are many food options and supermarkets. My child always says that the food at Sunway Hotel is better than elsewhere haha. If I travel to Malaysia again, Sunway Pyramid Hotel will be my choice for accommodation.
Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500

Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500

The bed was firm, yet comfortable, and the room was the perfect size. The breakfast/dining area was shared with the sister-hotel, Sunway Resort, and was quite tricky to find. But WOW -- it had the best selection of food choices I've seen in a breakfast buffet! We'd be very happy to stay at this hotel again.

Review: I had a seminar at Sunway Conference Centre so Sunway Pyramid Hotel was the best option to stay in. At check in time I request a middle room & high floor, both requests were fulfilled by a male concierge name Tuesday. I’m so grateful for his arrangement. Room was clean, bed is big. Toilet & bathroom is clean, just a little outdated with old stains. They provided 2bottles of glass filtered water for a single night stay which I truly appreciate. Buffer was at the next door Sunway Resort with great selections. Definitely worth the money.
Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500

Review: I recently stayed at Harbour Grand Kowloon and was impressed by the elegant ambience, spacious rooms, and stunning harbor views. The service was courteous and efficient throughout the stay, and the hotel’s facilities, including the rooftop pool and fitness center, were top-notch. However, the food was somewhat underwhelming. While the presentation was decent, the overall taste and variety did not quite meet the expectations set by the hotel’s otherwise high standards. There’s definitely room for improvement in the culinary department. besides, hotel amenities need to purchase separately as the hotel provide less of essential amenities like tooth brush , drinking water, etc. Still, if you’re looking for a comfortable and scenic stay in Hong Kong with great service and amenities, Harbour Grand Kowloon is a solid choice — just don’t expect the food to be a highlight.
Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500

Review: We checked in on 23/2 and initially had a pleasant experience. The facilities and overall environment were good, and our family, including our baby, were enjoying the stay. However, after returning to our room at night from shopping, we noticed a strong cigarette smell in the room. We immediately contacted the front desk to report the issue. We were informed that it might be due to the air circulation system and that nothing could be done at that time. Unfortunately, the cigarette smell persisted throughout the night and was still present the next morning around 7am+. This was particularly concerning for us as we had specifically booked a non-smoking room and are traveling with a baby. We believe that guests who reserve non-smoking rooms should be assured of a smoke-free environment. We kindly request that the hotel investigate this matter seriously and take the necessary steps to prevent similar situations in the future. Aside from this issue, the rest of our stay has been satisfactory. We hope the hotel will look into this matter and provide an appropriate response. Thank you for your attention.
Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500

Review: I had a great stay at Sunway Pyramid Hotel. The biggest advantage is its direct connection to Sunway Pyramid Mall—super convenient for shopping, food, and entertainment. While the hotel doesn’t have its own swimming pool, GHA Discovery members get complimentary access to the pool at Sunway Resort Hotel, which was a really nice bonus. There’s also free pool access at Sunway Lagoon Hotel, but be prepared for a 15-minute walk. One tip: be mindful of where you park. Preferred parking in Sunway is convenient but can cost twice the rate of regular parking per day. Overall, a comfortable and enjoyable stay, especially for those who want everything within walking distance.
Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500

Review: Hotel counter and lobby staff were very courteous and helpful despite the long checkin line on a busy Saturday. Room is simple and well kept. Super location if shopping at the mall. But traffic in and out of the hotel on a busy weekend was crazy.
Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500

Review: Got a surprise upgrade to Sunway Resort and all hand over process was done so smoothly. The suite was so spacious the kids were just excited about it. Easy access to Sunway Lagoon. Ultra busy traffic during breakfast at the coffee house but staff was very calm despite being given the 'black face' by customers. The only downside is I couldn't get a baby cot although I requested during booking, so yeah, I didn't sleep well. Bed was really comfy with enough pillows. Ooh and if you plan to stay late in Sunway Lagoon, which we did till 8ish pm, the direct exit to hotel is not 'available'. Would recommend to check on that before booking. We walked all the way through the mall, through another hotel n back to Sunway Resort after our water activities. Embarrassed.. hahaha. But still a very good stay for us.. thank you
Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500

Review: This is my second stay here. The room is clean and spacious. The hotel is strategically located next to the mall. The buffet is awesome, with a wide array of selections, ranging from pastry, to Asian style food. Definitely looking forward to stay back here when I have the chance.
Hotel_Address: Persiaran Lagoon, Bandar Sunway, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 47500

Review: Enjoyed our stay at Sunway Pyramid. Appreciated the adjoining rooms accorded to us, as can easily assist with the caring of my very young grandkids next door. Rooms were pleasant and clean, staff very helpfu and overall stay there was good service. Easily accessible to a massive shopping mall that housed a supermarket, numerous restaurants/fast food outlets, game zones, and even an ice skating ring. I didn't even need to utilise any transport from the hotel, apart from going to the airport, because most facilities/shoping items can be found within walking distance from our hotel. Breakfast at the hotel displayed a huge variety of dishes. Plus it was so easy to walk over to Sunway Lagoon for the activities.

Review: Sunway Pyramid hotel is a fantastic choice for both business and leisure travelers. The hotel boasts a prime location, offering easy access to shopping, dining, and cultural attractions. The rooms are spacious, modern, and well-appointed, ensuring a comfortable stay. The staff is exceptionally friendly and attentive, always going the extra mile to make guests feel welcome. Dining options are diverse, with delicious cuisine served at the on-site restaurants. The rooftop pool and fitness center are excellent additions for relaxation and staying active. Overall, Novotel Bangkok delivers exceptional value, comfort, and convenience—highly recommended!

Review: It was a pleasant stay at this hotel. The venue is easy to find and they have one of the nicest staff ever. The room was clean too! All amenities were provided and the best part about this hotel, it is link to the Sunway Pyramid Mall! Will definitely consider this hotel again in the future.

Guests are advised to use the complimentary swimming pool facilities at Sunway Hotel, or alternatively, access the pool at Sunway Resort for a fee which, in our view, was relatively costly.

Review: I recently stayed at the hotel for Pinkfish and had a mostly positive experience. The room was really, really nice – clean, spacious, and well-appointed with all the amenities we needed for a comfortable stay. The hotel's location is excellent, with a shopping mall downstairs making things very convenient for dining and shopping. However, we did encounter a significant inconvenience with the parking system. Despite paying the flat rate hotel guest parking fee at the lobby, when we tried to exit the parking lot, the system indicated that I hadn't made the payment. I called the hotel and was put on hold, which was frustrating. Ultimately, I had to walk all the way back to the lobby to resolve the issue. This was really, really inconvenient and put a damper on an otherwise pleasant stay. Overall, our stay was good, but I hope the hotel can improve their parking system to prevent similar issues for future guests because this is the second time this has happened to us.

Bed are king size & very comfortable, the pillow have few type which is Natural Buckwheat/Bamboo Charcoal/Memory Foam etc.. Coffee machine, espresso coffee, tea bags, 2 glass of water, 2 chocolate. Bath tub with jacuzzi and tv screen. I noticed that hotel provide Harman Kardon bluetooth speaker on the desk. Overall, it was great for me..

Review: Stayed at Sunway Pyramid Hotel mainly for easy access to Sunway Lagoon, and for that, it’s a solid choice. The good: The hotel is directly connected to Sunway Pyramid Mall, which is excellent for food and shopping – endless options. You can watch ice hockey for free in the evening if you’re interested – a fun bonus. Super close to Sunway Lagoon. In fact, you could even come back to your room in your wet clothes if you forget a change (okay, kidding… but technically true 😄). The not-so-good: Rooms are a bit small. Not many facilities inside the room Parking costs around 16 ringgit per day, which adds up Verdict: If you’re planning to spend most of your time at Sunway Lagoon or the mall, this is a very convenient base. Just don’t expect luxury space or free parking.

Review: Pros - A/C system are working perfectly - Body wash, shampoo and hand wash were provided - Basic dining sets are provided (without dishwashing soap) - Room is clean and tidy - Additional pillows were provided - WiFi system are working perfectly - 2 bottles of complimentary 500 mL drinking water were provided, and a selection of coffee and tea with sugar sachet as well - Customer service are excellent - A selection of grocery stores, restaurants and ATM are available at the ground floor area Cons - small shower room size - Stagnant water in the shower room during shower - Bed is not a real king size bed, it’s just a two single bed that was assembled together, thus there’s a tendency for the beds to be separated during resting - Kettle lid could not be closed - Exhaust fan in the shower room isn’t functioning - A smart TV is available inside the unit, but the TV response to the command that was transmitted from the remote control are very slow, and Netflix subscription is not available - Mini fridge is not so cool anymore - The room is not fully soundproofed, mainly on the window and entry door - Daily parking pass is not available, in comparison to their business counterpart (Greystone) that’s able to provide the aforementioned pass for a mere RM10 to their guest. Thus, Perfect Host guest has to either pay a hefty parking fee to secure an on site parking spot or to park their car at a public parking lot around the premise that comes with security concern - The tall cabinet that was situated beside the vanity table could not be closed/opened easily due to lack of space and poor installation

Review: The room itself is very comfortable but the surroundings could be better. It’s situated in a mall which is quite rundown, yet the parking rate is unexpectedly expensive. So, be careful for those who drives and want to park your car.
Hotel_name: Centrestage Petaling Jaya by Perfect Host
Hotel_Address: Centrestage Petaling Jaya by Perfect Host, P-G-05 No, 1, Jalan 13/1, Seksyen 13,46200 Petaling Jaya, Selangor., Petaling Jaya, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 46200

Review: harga bilik disini murah, untuk satu malam bawah rm100 pun boleh dapat. bilik bersih, aircond sejuk, cerek iron pun berfungsi cuma tv je tak boleh tengok. cuma ada 3 benda yang saya kurang puas hati. 1. Parking - Parking fee sangat mahal. Rm10 untuk first 8hours. Takkisahlah 30min pun still rm10. saya parking satu malam dari 730 malam hingga 8pagi total Rm23. Bila mak saya parking lepas hantar saya less than 1hour, dia charge rm25. Bila call asyik reject, whatsapp pun tak reply. 2. Environment Suram - Saya cadangkan untuk pasang lampu area menuju lif, dan kawasan luar. Sebab sini ramai foreigners, ada yang baik macam yang jaga kaunter. Tapi risau yang berlegar legar kat luar tu. Saya tunggu ibu saya parking kereta, saya duduk dekat sebelah KK Mart. Ada lelaki bangladesh berbaju biru jalan dan tenung saya. Dia patah balik sampai hujung jalan tapi masih asyik intai intai. Saya masuk KK pun dari jauh dia ikut. Tapi alhamdulillah selamat masuk lift dan ke bilik. 3. Kebersihan sekeliling - Lift agak berbau dan ada sampah tisu. Saya harap isu ni dapat diselenggara dan diselesaikan dalam masa terdekat.
Hotel_name: Centrestage Petaling Jaya by Perfect Host
Hotel_Address: Centrestage Petaling Jaya by Perfect Host, P-G-05 No, 1, Jalan 13/1, Seksyen 13,46200 Petaling Jaya, Selangor., Petaling Jaya, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 46200

Review: According to the out door parking charges table I received, the fee is RM10 for the first 8 hours, and RM3 for every additional hour. I parked from 10:00 PM until 9:18 AM the next morning, which is a total of 11 hours. Based on the stated charges, the total should be RM19.00 (RM10 for the first 8 hours + RM9 for the additional 3 hours). However, customer service charged me RM26.00. I was informed that after 12:00 AM, the charges reset for another 8-hour block, but this condition was not stated or declared in the parking charge schedule provided.
Hotel_name: Centrestage Petaling Jaya by Perfect Host
Hotel_Address: Centrestage Petaling Jaya by Perfect Host, P-G-05 No, 1, Jalan 13/1, Seksyen 13,46200 Petaling Jaya, Selangor., Petaling Jaya, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 46200

Review: If you want a short stay with minimum time spend at the hotel then i think its a fine place. But for holiday not a suitable place especially with small kids. They dont clean daily but once a week. So they dont provide fresh towel daily. The staff was friendly. When we ask for them they quickly attend to us. Its just that one of my rooms the toilet was leaking so it retainsed water on the floor. The tvs in both room didnt function. They provide iron in each room, kettle, plates, fork and spoon also basic amenities. Overall ok. But just for transit or short stay. The parking is freaking expensive and it is under different management so you cant waive the fee from the hotel stay.
Hotel_name: Centrestage Petaling Jaya by Perfect Host
Hotel_Address: Centrestage Petaling Jaya by Perfect Host, P-G-05 No, 1, Jalan 13/1, Seksyen 13,46200 Petaling Jaya, Selangor., Petaling Jaya, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 46200

Review: recommended place to stay for overnight or short stay for its affordable price. room is clean. got hot shower, iron, big mirror. Not suitable for solo woman to check in alone as it is quite dark and quiet from the lift to the rooms hallway. Not much lighting. Lift can be accessible to outsiders so it can be quite scary to ride the lift alone with unknown man. Reception is closed after 12 midnight until next 9 am. So if anything happened overnight, you are on your own. Security can be improved.
Hotel_name: Centrestage Petaling Jaya by Perfect Host
Hotel_Address: Centrestage Petaling Jaya by Perfect Host, P-G-05 No, 1, Jalan 13/1, Seksyen 13,46200 Petaling Jaya, Selangor., Petaling Jaya, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 46200

Review: Pros: The kitchenette was well-equipped, making it easy to cook or reheat food. All toiletries were provided, which was convenient and thoughtful. Cons: The room door was very heavy, requiring a strong slam to shut it properly—quite disturbing, especially at night. The soundproofing was poor, and I could clearly hear noises from the next room. There were also kids playing in the hallway, ringing doorbells, which got really annoying. Cleanliness was also an issue — I found hair inside the bed linen, and the pillow had a noticeable smell, which was off-putting. Lastly, the deposit refund took a very long time, which added unnecessary frustration after check-out. Overall, while there were some convenient features, the cleanliness and noise issues, along with the slow refund process, made the stay less than ideal.

Review: I hate to leave bad reviews for any hotel, however, I am compelled this time. I booked 3 nights on the wrong dates and immediately realized the mistake, called to change them and they said to book the correct dates and they would refund for the wrong booking. I then booked the correct dates, arrived to the hotel to sort it out and they said its not possible for them to cancel the dates because of Agoda's policies. Of course, I contacted Agoda, who submitted the cancel request to the hotel. The hotel ended up denying the claim and Agoda informed me of the hotel's decision. This wasn't Agoda's issue, the hotel denied the claim and lied straight to my face about it. At the end of the day I had to reverse this with my bank, which was easy considering the correspondances. This is no way to do business and people should be aware of this type of situation to avoid it. Wasted hours of my time over an attempt to deceive their guest.

My stay was disrupted by a very angry looking staff asking me about my check in after I had successfully checked Jin at the counter. Counter staff also was unsure of the deposit at check out. Review: ​I have mixed feelings about my stay. On the positive side, the bed was very comfortable and I slept well. However, the experience was ruined by several maintenance and service issues.​The Bad:​Power failures: The room blacked out twice, specifically every time I used the shower.​Service: During the first blackout, the team was fast. However, during the second blackout, I was stuck in the shower with soap in my eyes. The staff member who came didn't apologize and instead just told me it was time to checkout. It was extremely unprofessional and insensitive given the situation.​Cleanliness: Parts of the room were dusty, and the corridor had a distinct smell of cockroaches.​Amenities: No coffee was provided. There was a water heater, but strangely, no glass cups to use with it.​Parking: Free parking is available outside. If you park inside, you usually pay via Touch 'n Go, but the system was down during my stay, so I was forced to use the app.​I cannot recommend this place until they fix their electrical issues and train their staff on basic hospitality
Hotel_Address: Jalan 13/1, Petaling Jaya, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 46200

Review: Parking is expensive, my partner ended up parking outside the place, still within a walkable distance. This place requires a RM100 deposit, the staff will disappear around 12am, if you want to check out, need to leave your card on the counter, only after the staff come back in the morning they will process your deposit again a few days later. i also saw a dead cockroach in my room, kettle provided had the wrong plug, could not be used. luckily there is a mynews directly below the hotel so can get whatever you need from there. overall the location sucks, and parking isnt provided, there is also no 24 hours staffing. Would not recommend unless you are desperate and there are no other options. still a budget hotel.

Review: This hotel was conveniently located near the event we needed to attend, and initially, we could only book a twin single room. Upon our arrival, we requested an upgrade, explaining that my hubby hadn’t slept the night before due to event preps and that we would prefer a queen/king size bed if avail. We were open to paying extra for the upgrade, and the staff promptly facilitated this change, providing us with a very quiet room. The front desk staff was friendly, always smiling, and even checked in on us (on our way to dinner) to ensure everything was good. The room itself was cozy, equipped with all essential amenities, and super clean + comfy. The area offers some of the best nasi lemak and Ayam Gepuk I’ve had in a long time. The cafe next to the Ayam Gepuk place also serves excellent pastries. However..be sure to plan for parking, as the hotel doesn’t cover parking fees which I overlooked when making the booking.
Hotel_Address: 130, jalan radin anum, Bukit Jalil, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 57000

Review: The staff, room, a/c, bathroom were all new, clean and represented good value for money. Bed was comfy. No complaints at all. The location is interesting, its certainly not bad, there were heaps of shops and restaurants right near it, but it's about 9 to 10 km away from Kuala lumper city centre. So, if you want to see KL towers and the Petronis towers you'll have to use grab or catch public transport. Batu caves were probably about 25 km away. It's actually quite far from Kuala lumper airport which is about 50 minutes drive. So, it depends on what you want to do. Stay further in the heart of Kuala Lumper where you're nearer tourist attractions but will probably add another 20-25 mins drive time to airport or stay a little further out which makes the drive to the airport quicker and the hotel a bit cheaper. Still, I'm satisfied with my choice.

(I'm not sure of that) - Room cleanliness is okay, found molds around the toilet and still got hairs on the floor... luckily no weird smell in the room. - Only the rainfall shower head is working while the handheld one has no hot water supply and water pressure is too low. - Hoping the hotel management can consider improving the toilet cleanliness and fix the water pressure issue.
