Review: I want to start by giving credit where it’s due, the front desk staff are polite, patient, and available 24/7. Their attitude was good, and they genuinely tried their best to help whenever we approached them. However, the overall stay was disappointing due to the facilities and room conditions. First, the hotel does not have a lift, only a long staircase. This is extremely inconvenient for travellers with large or heavy luggage, especially after a long journey. This should be clearly highlighted beforehand. Facilities and room quality were below expectations. Room amenities are inconsistent, some rooms may have a fridge, some may not. My friend upgraded from a queen bedroom to a triple bedroom for an additional RM30 per night. While that room came with a mini fridge, it had a strong, unpleasant milk smell, likely left by previous guests. The bed was neatly arranged, but there were visible signs of dirtiness that should not be ignored. As for our queen-size bedroom, the space was extremely limited, with barely any room to move. The air conditioning was weak and did not cool the room properly. Although toiletries were provided in the toilet, there was no hair dryer in the room. Due to these issues, we checked out on the same night after returning from our travel, as we were not comfortable staying any longer. While the pricing is fair and cheap, it truly reflects the quality. You get what you pay for. The most disappointing part was the refund issue. Initially, the receptionist told us that as long as we requested a refund through Agoda, the hotel would accept it. However, after we checked out early and switched hotels, Agoda informed us that the property management refused the refund. We immediately called the hotel after receiving this message, but were told that it was “not their problem” since we chose to check out early, and they would not refund us.
Hotel_Address: 130, jalan radin anum, Bukit Jalil, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 57000

If you're waiting for a Grab you'll be waiting a long time most the time because of the jam on main road as well as on the side road leading to hotel. And if you've got a room facing this road, you won't get any rest (get earplugs). Room clearly not properly cleaned. Lots of hair and dust. Bathroom sink was scummy and rusty and lots of little cockroaches hiding under the sink. Quite off-putting. Staff are seemingly fine. Thank God for that. It's not exactly terrible. But you're better off paying a little bit more and try the next hotel on the list.

Review: Newly opened hotel with modern facilities and a convenient location (South). Paid around 3** ++ for a night. The room was simple yet big in size, equipped with a bed, sofa bed, working desk, chair, TV, and the basic amenities required. Cleanliness and hygiene standards were well maintained throughout the property. They have a shaded swimming pool, a breezy terrace area with a lot of tables and chairs perfect for relaxing. Car parking was charged at RM8 per day. For those who like to walk around, they have direct access to a shopping mall located at the ground floor just next door. Plenty of shops and dining options within walking distance too. Breakfast was enjoyable with a good variety of options, 7/10. What stood out most was the service provided by the staff. Mr. Nathan & the restaurant team delivered welcoming customer service and were attentive to diners' needs. Not to forget, the Marketing Manager Ms. Wee Wee (name might be spelled wrong, she’s a Chinese woman) and Assistant Exec Housekeeper Mr. Chong, who were present at the restaurant during breakfast hour, went the extra mile in providing a pleasant service recovery and showed genuine care. Mr. Devin & Ms. Diane from Front Office just did their job efficiently, ensuring that the check-in and the guest experience were handled smoothly. They were approachable and willing to assist with guests' inquiries and requests. The luggage delivery to the room experienced a slight delay. However, the matter was handled after a phone call. The Security guards stationed at the entrance showed great hospitality. They always greeted guests with smiles, promptly opened doors & maintained a welcoming presence, contributing positively to guest experience from the moment of arrival. Overall, it was a relaxing one-day stay with good facilities, convenient surroundings, and a team that showed efforts in delivering quality service. Keep improving

Food variety: Menu for in room dining is limited I would say but for breakfast it was good - both variety and taste wise. I cant remember the exact name but I found the parata/roti canai with the dal and vegetarian curry was good! The fruits are fresh too.

Review: Overall the stay was okay. Room was spacious and clean, bed was comfy (my son likes it very much). We asked for late checkout and was accomodated by the front desk officer, really appreciated as our flight was at 5pm. One of the bathroom light was broken and respond from Engineering team was fast, change with new lightbulb and working perfectly. Amenities provided in the bathroom is complete, shower gel and shampoo smells very nice. Breakfast was also good with may selections, and I think all the food is halal. At check out time, the concierge team also very helpful, offering to order Grab for us to the airport. Another advantage of staying here is the shopping mall located in the same building and there is a direct access elevator from 3rd floor of hotel to the shopping mall. There is a 24 hours supermarket at the shopping mall and many selections for food & drinks, it even have an indoor children playground. The only minus is location of hotel is not in the city center, we will need to drive approximately 30 minutes to city center if your plan is to visit the Twin Tower or KL Tower.

Review: Newly opened hotel with modern facilities and a convenient location (South). Paid around 3** ++ for a night. The room was simple yet big in size, equipped with a bed, sofa bed, working desk, chair, TV, and the basic amenities required. Cleanliness and hygiene standards were well maintained throughout the property. They have a shaded swimming pool, a breezy terrace area with a lot of tables and chairs perfect for relaxing. Car parking was charged at RM8 per day. For those who like to walk around, they have direct access to a shopping mall located at the ground floor just next door. Plenty of shops and dining options within walking distance too. Breakfast was enjoyable with a good variety of options, 7/10. What stood out most was the service provided by the staff. Mr. Nathan & the restaurant team delivered welcoming customer service and were attentive to diners' needs. Not to forget, the Marketing Manager Ms. Wee Wee (name might be spelled wrong, she’s a Chinese woman) and Assistant Exec Housekeeper Mr. Chong, who were present at the restaurant during breakfast hour, went the extra mile in providing a pleasant service recovery and showed genuine care. Mr. Devin & Ms. Diane from Front Office just did their job efficiently, ensuring that the check-in and the guest experience were handled smoothly. They were approachable and willing to assist with guests' inquiries and requests. The luggage delivery to the room experienced a slight delay. However, the matter was handled after a phone call. The Security guards stationed at the entrance showed great hospitality. They always greeted guests with smiles, promptly opened doors & maintained a welcoming presence, contributing positively to guest experience from the moment of arrival. Overall, it was a relaxing one-day stay with good facilities, convenient surroundings, and a team that showed efforts in delivering quality service. Keep improving.
Hotel_name: Courtyard by Marriott Kuala Lumpur South

Review: Overall the stay was okay. Room was spacious and clean, bed was comfy (my son likes it very much). We asked for late checkout and was accomodated by the front desk officer, really appreciated as our flight was at 5pm. One of the bathroom light was broken and respond from Engineering team was fast, change with new lightbulb and working perfectly. Amenities provided in the bathroom is complete, shower gel and shampoo smells very nice. Breakfast was also good with may selections, and I think all the food is halal. At check out time, the concierge team also very helpful, offering to order Grab for us to the airport. Another advantage of staying here is the shopping mall located in the same building and there is a direct access elevator from 3rd floor of hotel to the shopping mall. There is a 24 hours supermarket at the shopping mall and many selections for food & drinks, it even have an indoor children playground. The only minus is location of hotel is not in the city center, we will need to drive approximately 30 minutes to city center if your plan is to visit the Twin Tower or KL Tower.
Hotel_name: Courtyard by Marriott Kuala Lumpur South

Review: We stayed at this hotel for seven nights. The one thing we genuinely liked was the bed. It was very comfortable and helped us sleep better, which is not something we usually experience. Unfortunately, the overall service and facilities were disappointing. Room service was very slow. We requested forks and spoons and had to wait a long time. We also asked for a refill of toilet paper in the bathroom, but the reception said they usually do not provide refills, which we found unreasonable for a hotel stay. We had to change rooms once due to a sewage pipe issue that caused a strong smell in the room. The reception promised to send staff to assist us with moving rooms, but we waited a long time before any help arrived. It seemed the hotel might be short-staffed. Housekeeping also came at different times each day, which made it difficult to plan our schedule. The swimming pool is poorly designed, with sharp edges and very slippery tiles that lack proper anti-slip texture. The pool is not heated, and the water was too cold to swim comfortably. The swimming facilities also feel incomplete, as there is no outdoor shower area. In addition, the steam room and sauna in the female restroom were not working during our stay. Overall, while the bed was comfortable, the service, facilities, and management need significant improvement.
Hotel_name: Courtyard by Marriott Kuala Lumpur South
Hotel_Address: 137 Jalan Puchong, Bukit Jalil, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 58200

Review: A new hotel, so the facilities are very clean and well maintained. Good location with a reasonable mall attached. Rooms are a good size with suitable amenities and nicely designed. Great view of KL too. However, check in is less than ideal, esoecially if you have young children with you. I was fortunate to arrive early (around 2pm) so I checked in quickly and was allowed to go to the pool area with my son to wait until my room was ready. When the time came to go to my room (3.30pm) I was told by lobby staff that I needed to queue up again (even though I had checked in already). The wait was at least 45 minutes going by the 25 or so people already in the queue and waiting in the lobby. I refused and eventually got my keycard to go to the room. Car parking is also confusing and when I requested to validate my car registration number, at 4.45pm, to avail of the hotel rate, I was told again I had to queue up (similar sized queue as at 3.30pm). Check out was the easiest experience, with no queue (I checked out earlier than 12noon to avoid potential queues). Staff were stony faced, not at all friendly and did not even ask if I had enjoyed my stay. This is disappointing for a hotel that is part of the Marriott group and not at all how I imagined my experience to be. Overall, a very nice hotel, with mediocre customer service.
Hotel_name: Courtyard by Marriott Kuala Lumpur South
Hotel_Address: 137 Jalan Puchong, Bukit Jalil, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, 58200

Review: I booked a room for my grandparents as we had an event in the Old Klang Road area. While the room was generally clean, the mirror had noticeable fingerprints, which required me to call housekeeping for cleaning. The bed was comfortable, but the bathroom could benefit from a hand bidet, which would enhance overall comfort. Additionally, the hotel should consider offering more assistance for older guests, such as wheelchairs or other mobility aids. One major disappointment was the lack of suitable entertainment options for my grandparents. The television didn’t offer any Tamil or Indian channels, and since it wasn’t a smart TV, we were unable to connect to YouTube. The hotel expected us to bring our own HDMI cable, which I found to be unreasonable for a five-star establishment. I expected better amenities and more thoughtful entertainment options.

We've to take Grab everywhere as it's not near city centre, bus terminal etc. The average waiting time to get a Grab was 7 mins, with once being 12 mins.

Review: I stayed here few times previously and it has been a great experience so far. However, the bedsheet was also a bit smelly / musty smell but wasnt as bad as the first room. didnt bother to change because it seems that they don't have any other room left and it's full house. it's always quiet here but this time, a group of 10++ was in the room besides me and being so loud at night. Review: I recently stayed at this hotel and had an unforgettable experience. The service exceeded my expectations, starting with a complimentary room upgrade which was a pleasant surprise. The staff were incredibly friendly and welcoming. The room was beautiful and impeccably clean, and the entire hotel was elegant and spotless. I would like to extend a special thanks to Mr. Afif, the receptionist, for his courteous and professional service. He was extremely helpful and assisted me with everything I needed. Additionally, the late check-out until 4 PM was a fantastic perk that allowed me to enjoy my stay even longer. Overall, this was an exceptional stay and I highly recommend this hotel to anyone seeking a luxurious and comfortable experience.
